en.extract:
The Tagula butcherbird (Cracticus louisiadensis) is a species of bird in the family Artamidae.
It is endemic to the lowland forests of Tagula Island in Papua New Guinea. It occupies less than 800km2 and has an estimated population of 11,500 to 23,000 individuals.


Taxonomy
The species was first described by Henry Baker Tristram in 1889. It is monotypic, with no recognized subspecies. 


Description
The bird is described as large.
The plumage is mainly black, with white patches on the shoulder, wing, and tail.

The '''Tagula butcherbird''' ('''''Cracticus louisiadensis''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Artamidae]].
It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to the lowland forests<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Tagula Butcherbird - eBird |url=https://ebird.org/species/tagbut1 |archive-url=http://web.archive.org/web/20250907052458/https://ebird.org/species/tagbut1 |archive-date=2025-09-07 |access-date=2026-03-25 |website=ebird.org |language=en}}</ref> of [[Tagula Island]] in [[Papua New Guinea]]. It occupies less than 800km<sup>2</sup> and has an estimated population of 11,500 to 23,000 individuals.<ref>Goulding, W., Moss, P., & McAlpine, C. A. [https://eprints.qut.edu.au/250970/ Notes on the cultural value, biology and conservation status of the Data Deficient Tagula butcherbird (Cracticus louisiadensis Tristram, 1889) | QUT ePrints], ''eprints.qut.edu.au.'' Retrieved 2025-09-11</ref>

== Taxonomy ==
The species was first described by Henry Baker Tristram in 1889. It is monotypic, with no recognized subspecies. <ref>{{Cite journal |last=Russell |first=Eleanor |last2=Rowley |first2=Ian |last3=Sharpe |first3=Christopher J. |date=2020 |title=Tagula Butcherbird (Cracticus louisiadensis), version 1.0 |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/tagbut1/cur/introduction |journal=Birds of the World |language=en |doi=10.2173/bow.tagbut1.01 |issn=2771-3105|url-access=subscription }}</ref>

== Description ==
The bird is described as large.

The plumage is mainly black, with white patches on the shoulder, wing, and tail.<ref name=":0" />
